title: Perfectionism Thought Record
description: Use this worksheet to identify and challenge perfectionistic thoughts using cognitive restructuring. Examine the evidence for and against your rigid rules and develop more balanced, flexible alternatives.
instructions: When you notice a perfectionistic thought causing distress, work through each field to examine the thought carefully and develop a more balanced perspective.

fields.label: 1. Situation — What was happening?
fields.placeholder: Describe the situation briefly (e.g., 'Finishing a work presentation,' 'Received feedback on a project'). Please avoid including names or identifying details.

fields.label: 2. Perfectionistic Thought — What went through your mind?
fields.placeholder: Write the exact thought (e.g., 'If this presentation is not perfect, everyone will think I am incompetent')

fields.label: 5. Cognitive Distortion — Which pattern is this?
6. Evidence For — What facts support this thought?
fields.placeholder: List actual evidence (not feelings) that supports the thought

fields.label: 7. Evidence Against — What contradicts this thought?
fields.placeholder: Consider: Has this fear come true before? What would I say to a friend? Am I confusing a feeling with a fact?

fields.label: 8. Balanced Thought — What is a more realistic perspective?
fields.placeholder: Write a realistic, evidence-based alternative that acknowledges nuance (not a positive affirmation)

distortionsList.name: All-or-Nothing Thinking
distortionsList.description: Everything is either perfect or terrible, success or failure, with no middle ground. 'If it is not 100%, it is a failure.'

distortionsList.name: Should/Must Statements
distortionsList.description: Rigid rules disguised as universal truths. 'I should always be productive.' 'I must never make mistakes.'

distortionsList.name: Catastrophizing
distortionsList.description: Imagining extreme, irreversible consequences for mistakes. 'If I make a typo, my career is over.'

distortionsList.name: Discounting the Positive
distortionsList.description: Dismissing achievements, compliments, or evidence of competence. 'They are just being nice.' 'That was not hard enough to count.'

distortionsList.name: Mental Filtering
distortionsList.description: Focusing exclusively on the one imperfect element while ignoring everything that went well.

distortionsList.name: Emotional Reasoning
distortionsList.description: Treating feelings as evidence of facts. 'I feel like a failure, therefore I am a failure.'

disclaimer: This worksheet is for educational purposes and self-understanding. It is not a substitute for professional mental health care. If perfectionistic thinking is causing significant distress, please consider working with a qualified therapist.
